hero.title: Clinical training for the
hero.subtitle: Practice therapy with AI-simulated patients. A safe environment to learn, make mistakes, and grow.
howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three steps to start developing your clinical skills
howItWorks.step1Desc: Select from patients with different backgrounds, personalities, and levels of clinical difficulty.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Practice your therapeutic skills in a natural conversation. The patient reacts to your approach.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Receive feedback on your session and progressively develop your clinical competencies.
patients.title: Meet the patients
patients.subtitle: {count} patients with unique backstories, defined personalities, and realistic reactions to different therapeutic approaches
features.title: Why choose GlorIA
features.subtitle: Tools designed for 21st-century clinical training
features.feature1Desc: Each patient has unique personality traits, defense mechanisms, and emotional responses. They open up if they trust you, and shut down if you rush them.
features.feature1Highlight3: Gradual disclosures based on therapeutic alliance
features.feature2Desc: Practice without fear of making mistakes. Explore different therapeutic approaches, make errors, and learn from them without real-world consequences.
features.feature3Desc: At the end of each session, receive a reflection on your performance. Identify strengths and areas for improvement in your clinical skills.
comparison.title: GlorIA vs. traditional methods
comparison.subtitle: How AI patient practice compares to traditional peer role-play.
comparison.row2Traditional: Depends on your partner's acting ability
comparison.row2Gloria: AI trained with validated clinical profiles
comparison.row3Traditional: Limited to what your partner can improvise
comparison.row4Traditional: Depends on having a supervisor present
comparison.row4Gloria: Automatic assessment of 10 competencies at the end
comparison.row5Traditional: Hard to recreate the same scenario twice
comparison.row5Gloria: Same patient, same story, multiple attempts
comparison.row6Gloria: Zero risk — the patient is AI
comparison.row7Gloria: Dashboard with metrics, XP, and full history
comparison.row10Gloria: Access to transcripts and post-session evaluations
comparison.footnote: GlorIA complements traditional clinical training. Human supervision and in-person practice remain essential.
impact.title: Results that back the platform
impact.subtitle: Real data from pilots and comparative studies demonstrating the effectiveness of GlorIA's adaptive clinical engine
impact.findingsSubtitle: 3 therapist levels (basic, intermediate, advanced) x 8 sessions with the same patient
impact.finding1Desc: Advanced therapists reduce patient resistance 3.5 times more than basic therapists over 8 sessions.
impact.finding2Desc: The alliance built by advanced therapists is 2.7 times stronger than with directive interventions.
impact.finding3Desc: Therapists who give premature advice show no improvement in the patient's symptomatology in any session.
impact.finding4Desc: The patient reacts if the therapist stays silent for more than 90 seconds, just like in real life.
impact.footnote: Data from the comparative study of therapeutic levels with patient Roberto Salas (52 years old, grief) using GlorIA's adaptive engine with NLP intervention classifier, semantic RAG, and Memory-Context Processing (MCP).
testimonials.title: What our students say
testimonials.subtitle: Experiences from those who already train with GlorIA
testimonials.quote1: Before GlorIA, my only practice was role-playing with classmates. Talking with a patient who actually reacts to what I say changed the way I understand therapy.
testimonials.quote2: It helped me a lot with managing silences and resistant patients. With Carmen, I learned that pushing doesn't work, and I took that lesson into my professional practice.
testimonials.quote3: I use it before every practical exam. I can repeat the session, try different approaches, and see how the patient's response changes. It's like a flight simulator for therapists.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about GlorIA before you start practicing.
faq.a1: No. All GlorIA patients are fictional characters simulated by artificial intelligence, with personalities and clinical conditions designed by mental health professionals. Their faces and voices are also AI-generated.
faq.a2: Yes. Your sessions are private and only you and the instructors authorized by your institution can access them for academic purposes. We never share the content of your conversations with third parties outside the training context.
faq.q3: What happens if I say something wrong in a session?
faq.a3: Nothing bad. GlorIA is designed for you to practice and learn from your mistakes in a safe environment with no real consequences. The AI patient will react realistically, allowing you to observe the impact of your interventions and improve.
faq.q4: Can the AI patient react to what I say?
faq.a4: Yes. Each patient responds in real time based on their personality, life story, and the conversation context. If you show empathy, they'll notice. If you interrupt them, they'll notice that too. It's a realistic practice experience.
faq.a5: At the end of each session, the AI evaluates 10 clinical competencies based on an instrument developed by Universidad Gabriela Mistral, inspired by therapeutic competency models such as Roth and Pilling (2007), the APA framework for clinical psychology competencies, and Lambert and Barley's (2001) common factors. Competencies include active listening, affect containment, therapeutic setting, unconditional positive regard (Rogers, 1957), among others.
faq.q6: Can I use GlorIA from my phone?
faq.a6: Yes. GlorIA works in any modern browser, including Chrome and Safari on mobile devices. You don't need to install any app.
faq.q7: Can my university see my sessions?
faq.a7: Authorized instructors can see your progress, evaluations, and competencies as part of the training process. Conversation details can be reviewed for pedagogical purposes, just like in-person clinical supervision.
faq.q8: Do I need to install anything?
faq.a8: No. GlorIA works directly in your web browser. You only need an internet connection and an active institutional account.
faq.q9: Is it ethical to practice with AI patients?
faq.a9: Yes. AI simulation follows the same ethical principles as role-playing and standardized patients used in medical training for decades. GlorIA enables practice without risk to real people, reduces premature exposure of students to complex cases, and offers a safe space to make mistakes and learn. All clinical scenarios are fictional and designed under professional supervision. We comply with Chile's Data Protection Law 21.719 and the principles of Chile's AI legislation project.
faq.q10: Does AI replace instructors or clinical supervisors?
faq.a10: No. GlorIA is a complementary tool. Human supervision remains essential in clinical training. The platform expands practice opportunities between supervision sessions, but AI evaluations are guidance-oriented and do not replace the professional judgment of the instructor.
